Once they were injected into the living mice, the composites were excreted specifically through kidney, temporarily accumulates in the bladder. And then the composites react with aminopeptidase N to turn on the infrared fluorescence signal, indicating the presence of bladder cancer (Fig. 10 ). Another work the same team [218] developed was hemicyanine fluorophore caged with the main protease peptide substrate combined with β-CD by covalent connection for urinalysis of SARS-CoV-2. Although the two reports use the same infrared fluorescence signaling moiety, changing different biomarker-reactive moieties to achieve sensing and in vivo bioimaging can successfully enrich the application of FC-CDs in vitro imaging.

Composites of fluorescent probes have many applications, of which the development of FC-CDs in recent years undoubtedly is the most surprising. Preparing FC-CDs by different kinds of preparation strategies endows some properties compared with single fluorescent probes, like enhancing the fluorescence intensity, biocompatibility and chemical stability of the fluorescent probes. FC-CDs sensors show high selectivity and sensitivity to various targets such as inorganic ions, organic pollutants, biomolecules, temperature and pH, which are conducive to fluorescence sensing and imaging applications.

Despite a plenty of reports about FC-CDs have been discussed and successfully used in the field of sensing and bioimaging, we believe that there are still lots of room for research and improvement of FC-CDs in the future. Firstly, although some researches about bioimaging have been reported, it is still challenging for FC-CDs to be used in bioimaging fields. The environment of the organism is relatively complex. Because the biocompatibility of MNCs and QDs are slightly poor and the water solubility of ODs are insufficient, there possibly exists the interference of biological tissue autofluorescence and other issues, which are still obstacles for FC-CDs to enter cells or organisms. How to make the composites become more possess biocompatibility and applied in biological imaging becoming the promising research.

Secondly, theoretical tools such as computational chemistry, theoretical computation and chemometrics have been rarely employed in this field, which may be helpful to get the structure accurately and determine the best synthesis conditions of FC-CDs. Thus, developing theoretical tools is an effective way for the further study of FC-CDs.

Thirdly, up to now, most of the current researches have been focused on the detection of single ions or molecules. The ability of detecting multiple ions or molecules sensitively and simultaneously is also the key to improve the performance of the composites.

Next, fluorescence spectrums of FC-CDs are basically single emission peaks. The single emission peak is easily affected by environmental factors, causing the deviation of the results. Whether they are possible to combine with other materials, especially fluorescent materials to get double emission peaks. Besides, continuous efforts are required to develop multi-mode sensing strategies to obtain satisfactory results.

Last but not least, for further applications, some FC-CDs can be combined with smartphones or other micro equipment to speed up the results of detection and provide attractive portability, especially in areas related to environmental pollutants sensing. It is of great importance to design smart drug delivery systems that can release drugs under different disease/physiological conditions. CDs have relatively hydrophobic inner cavities and hydrophilic outer surfaces covered with hydroxyl groups. Due to the existence of such a hydrophobic cavity, some anticancer and antitumor drugs such as paclitaxel, doxorubicin hydrochloride and methotrexate can be encapsulated in FC-CDs to construct drug delivery and cancer therapy systems, which can broaden the application of drug delivery and therapy effectively. Besides, whether FC-CDs can be constructed and applied multiply in other fields rather than bio-related fields, such as catalysis, light emitting diode, etc., which is also a question worth pondering.

In summary, FC-CDs have shown great properties and wide applications in sensing and bioimaging, and we believe that better performance is expected in the future of FC-CDs for a wider range of applications including drug slow release, antibiosis and catalysis.

ce:biography.ce:simple-para: Yiqi Li is a master student of Analytical Chemistry in College of Chemistry of Jilin University. Her research direction is focused on fluorescence biosensing.
Songrui Li is a a doctoral student of Analytical Chemistry in College of Chemistry of Jilin University. Her research direction is focused on fluorescent biosensors based on metal nanoclusters.

Zhenzhen Huang is a professor of Analytical Chemistry in College of Chemistry of Jilin University. Her research interests are focused on the design and preparation of high-performance nanoprobes and their applications in biochemical analysis and detection.

Dawei Zhang is a professor of Analytical Chemistry in College of Chemistry of Jilin University. His research interests are focused on the development of novel materials for solid-phase (micro) extraction of trace analytes, synthetic chemistry of the analytical material and application.

Qiong Jia is a professor of Analytical chemistry in College of Chemistry of Jilin University. Her research interests include: 1) Developments of sensor-based analytical methods; 2) Offline & Online separation & preconcentration methods including solid phase extraction, microextraction, and magnetic strategies.
